abstract: This entry is a metadata record for a 2023 collaborative paper by Bruns, Bechmann, and colleagues that revisits foundational concepts in digital media research—specifically influence, populism, partisanship, and polarisation. No abstract or full text was available for summarisation, so substantive content cannot be reported.
key_claims: Not available: no abstract or body text was provided to extract claims from.
methods: Not available from the supplied metadata.
findings: Not available from the supplied metadata.
contributions: Signalled by the title: a conceptual revisiting of four key terms in digital media research—influence, populism, partisanship, and polarisation—though specifics cannot be confirmed without the text.
framing: Based on the title and author list (which spans scholars associated with the QUT Digital Media Research Centre and collaborators in Europe and the US), the paper appears positioned within digital media and communication studies, engaging debates about how core analytic concepts travel into platform and computational research contexts.
